roles.heading: Roles
roles.description: Job profiles evaluated against your model. Roles, never persons.
roles.empty: No roles yet. Create your first role to start evaluating.

create.description: The basics first. Purpose and responsibilities can be drafted with AI on the role page.

family.removeHint: Roles keep all their data; they just leave the family.

detail.profileIncomplete: Fill in purpose and responsibilities before rating.
detail.archivedHint: This role is archived and read-only. Its history is kept.

archive.error: The role could not be archived. Try again.

anchor.motivationPlaceholder: Why is this role a good reference point?
anchor.requiresAssessment: The role needs a completed assessment before it can become an anchor role.
anchor.countHint: 2 to 5 anchor roles in total is usually enough.
anchor.tooMany: You already have {count} active anchor roles. 2 to 5 in total is usually enough.
anchor.error: The change could not be saved. Try again.

toolbar.noMatches: No roles match the search or filters.

result.farFromAnchors: This result is at least two bands from the nearest anchor role. Validate the assessment manually before approving.

rating.lockedExplanation: The rating is locked because the role is approved or archived. Reopen the role from the role page to rate it again.

bands.heading: Overview
bands.description: The bands and the roles in each, derived live from the current model and ratings.
bands.pendingDescription: These roles have no band until their assessment is complete.
bands.deviationLabel: Computed band deviates from the agreed band {band}
bands.empty: No roles to show yet. Create a role and evaluate it to see the overview.

invitation.signInFirst: Sign in to accept this invitation.

auth.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

onboarding.waitingForAdmin: Your organization is still being set up by an administrator.

organization.heading: Create your organization
organization.description: The organization holds your roles, your evaluation model, and your team.
organization.error: The organization could not be created. Try again.

families.heading: Role families and roles at {name}
families.pasteHelpBody: Paste straight from a document, spreadsheet or HR system; the AI works out what you mean. If your text already mentions role families they are kept, otherwise the AI suggests which roles belong together in a family. You review the result and can change everything before anything is created.
families.placeholderPhrase1: Engineering: Software Developer, Tech Lead, Engineering Manager
families.placeholderPhrase2: Accountant, Payroll Specialist, HR Generalist, Office Manager
families.placeholderPhrase3: Store Manager, Sales Associate, Visual Merchandiser
families.dndInstructions: Press space or enter to pick up a role, use the arrow keys to move it, and press space or enter again to drop it. Press escape to cancel.
families.reviewHint: Group the roles into families and set each role's track.
families.reviewHintStarter: A starting point from your industry: group roles into families and set each role's track. Change anything later.
families.prefillingBody: We're writing a purpose and responsibilities for each role from its name. This can take a moment for a large set.

score.forkHeading: Your roles are ready. Want to evaluate them now?
score.scoreNowDescription: Evaluate your roles now and see their results.
score.laterDescription: Go to your dashboard and pick this up anytime.
score.saveExitLine: You can save and exit anytime and continue in your dashboard.
score.captureHint: A band needs a short written basis. Fill in the purpose and responsibilities to continue.
score.captureHintPrefilled: We drafted this purpose and these responsibilities from the role name. Edit anything before you evaluate.
score.doneBody: You have evaluated every role. You can refine them anytime in your dashboard.

model.heading: Choose your evaluation model
model.description: The model defines the criteria roles are rated against. Each criterion gets 1 to 5 weight points within a fixed point budget.

template.title: Start from the standard template
template.description: 9 ready-made criteria with assessment anchors and a balanced weighting. Everything can be adjusted afterwards.

scratch.title: Build from scratch
scratch.description: Start empty and define your own criteria, with AI drafts to help.

review.heading: {name}'s model
review.description: Review the criteria and their weighting. Everything can be changed later.

editor.heading: Criteria
editor.empty: No criteria yet. Add your first criterion or generate AI drafts.
editor.name: Name
editor.levelsIntro: Describe what a role looks like at each level, from 0 (lowest) to 5 (highest).
editor.pointsLeft: {count, plural, one {# weight point} other {# weight points}} left to distribute
editor.pointsOver: {count, plural, one {# weight point} other {# weight points}} over the budget
editor.minCriteriaHint: Add at least {min} criteria to continue.
editor.removalFloorHint: A model needs at least {min} criteria, so criteria cannot be removed right now.
editor.addDialogDescription: Describe what the criterion measures and write its assessment anchors. New criteria start at weight 3.
editor.editDialogDescription: Adjust the texts to your organization. The weight and existing ratings are not affected.
editor.removeDialogDescription: The criterion's ratings are removed from every role and its weight points are redistributed across the remaining criteria. This cannot be undone.

ai.heading: AI assistance
ai.provenance: AI-generated suggestion. Review and confirm; nothing is applied automatically.
ai.noMoves: The AI found no moves to suggest.
ai.moveLabel: Move {points, plural, one {# weight point} other {# weight points}} from {from} to {to}

help.criterionBody: A criterion is something every role is rated against, like scope or complexity. Anchor texts describe what the ratings 0 to 5 mean for it, and its weight points decide how much it counts in the overall weighting.
help.anchorsBody: These are the six levels, 0 to 5, for this criterion. Each one describes what a role looks like at that level: write them concrete and role-focused, clearly increasing from 0 (not present) to 5 (the strongest reasonable expression), so two assessors reading the same job profile land on the same rating. Assessment levels are texts on a single criterion; an anchor role is a whole reference role used to compare assessments.
help.anchorRoleBody: An anchor role is a reference role your organization has assessed and agreed on. Use the few you designate (2 to 5 is usually enough) to sanity-check other assessments: the comparison supports a decision, it never replaces the assessment. Anchor roles are whole roles; the assessment anchors on each criterion are descriptions of the ratings.
help.trackBody: A track is the kind of job, for example individual contributor or manager. It is set on the role and groups roles with the same kind of responsibility. It says nothing about seniority or how high the role lands.
help.familyBody: A role family groups roles that share the same kind of work, like Engineering or Sales. Families organize the role register and the results filter; they never affect the weighting.
help.weightPointsBody: Every criterion gets 1 to 5 weight points from one fixed budget for the whole model, and the percentage share is computed from the points. Giving one criterion more weight therefore means others count relatively less. New criteria start at the neutral 3.
help.scoreLabel: How are the weighting and band computed?
help.scoreBody: Every criterion is rated 0 to 5 and weighted by its weight points; the total is normalized to a fixed 0 to 100 scale, the weighting. The band then follows automatically from the weighting via the model's band thresholds, where band 1 is the highest. Neither can be set by hand.
help.blindRatingBody: Pick the anchor text that best matches what the role requires, not how the current employee performs. Ratings, weighting, and band stay hidden until the last criterion so each one is judged on its own; the result appears as soon as every criterion is rated.
help.onboardingScoreLabel: What does evaluating a role do?
help.onboardingScoreBody: Evaluating rates the role against each criterion in your model. The band is derived from those ratings on a fixed scale, never set by hand, so roles stay comparable. You rate one criterion at a time without seeing the running result.
help.statusBody: A role moves from draft to in review to approved. Approving locks the profile and the ratings as reviewed; reopen the role to change them again. The status never affects the computed result.
help.familiesReviewBody: A role family groups roles that share the same kind of work, like Engineering or Sales; families organize the register and never affect the weighting. A track is the kind of job, for example individual contributor or manager; it says nothing about seniority.
help.pendingBandLabel: Why does a role have no band yet?
help.pendingBandBody: A role gets a band only after every criterion is rated. Until the assessment is complete it has no weighting and no band, so it waits in this list.
help.contributionLabel: What does the contribution bar show?
help.contributionBody: Each criterion's share of this role's weighting: your rating times the criterion's weight in the model. The bars are relative to the biggest contributor.

errors.notAMember: You are not a member of this organization.
errors.adminRequired: Only organization admins can do this.
errors.membershipConflict: Your membership could not be verified. Contact support.
errors.modelExists: This organization already has an evaluation model.
errors.aiUnavailable: AI is not configured for this environment.
errors.aiGenerationFailed: The AI suggestion could not be generated.
errors.roleLocked: This role is approved and locked. Reopen it first.
errors.ratingsIncomplete: All criteria must be rated first.
errors.invalidTransition: That status change is not allowed.
errors.roleFamilyExists: A family with that name already exists.
errors.weightsUnbalanced: The weighting must match the point budget.
errors.tooFewCriteria: A model needs at least 5 criteria.
